violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Employees must register in and successfully complete an approved course in food safety management. Once completed, one employee must register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

Bean sprouts and eggs observed held at 47 - 48 degrees Fahrenheit on a cart at room temperature. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food products were removed from refrigeration 2 hours prior to inspection. Bean sprouts and eggs were returned to refrigeration units. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients observed not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed in the basement. No food/food equipment observed to be contaminated. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Areas of droppings must be properly cleaned and sanitized in order to inspect for new activity. Establishment has active pest control. Pest control service is to be increased as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025
Onions were observed stored on the floor on the staircase. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025
Rice spoons were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils must be stored in a clean, dry location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of equipment (gaskets, shelving) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2025
The floor in the kitchen is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024

Take-out containers, paper products, and unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement against the walls. Foods and equipment must be away from the walls and separated from each other in order to adequately inspect for pest activity. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Employee(s) must register/successfully complete approved course in food safety management. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2024

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a tray of food. In addition, no soap was available at this sink. A hand-washing sink shall always be supplied with soap and must be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed around the perimeter of the basement and in the food storage area. No foods/equipment observed to be adulterated. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Areas of droppings must be thoroughly cleaned in order to observe any new activity. Establishment has active pest control. Pest control service to be increased as needed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigeration units (outer surfaces, gaskets, shelving) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

The floor throughout the kitchen is in poor repair. Some progress has been made at the cook/grill line. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2024

Take-out containers, paper products, unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed against the walls of the basement. Products must be away from the walls in order to be able to inspect for pest activity. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024
The hood vents above the cook/grill line observed to have an accumulation of grease build-up and must be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 02/16/2024
